name: container-orchestration
description: Docker and Kubernetes patterns. Triggers on: Dockerfile, docker-compose, kubernetes, k8s, helm, pod, deployment, service, ingress, container, image.
capabilities: Generate secure Dockerfile templates with multi-stage builds
Write Kubernetes Deployment, Service, and Ingress manifests
Build Helm chart structures with values files
Configure health checks and resource limits
author: 0xDarkMatter
securitySummary: This skill provides documentation and templates for container orchestration. Static findings are predominantly false positives: the 'rm -rf' flags target standard apt cache cleanup paths (/var/lib/apt/lists/*), not root directories. Template placeholders use standard ${VAR:-default} syntax without actual credential exposure. Documentation patterns and script commands are controlled and appropriate for a DevOps skill. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or unauthorized access patterns confirmed.
valueStatement: Creating container deployments requires understanding Docker best practices, Kubernetes manifests, and Helm charts. This skill provides production-ready patterns and templates for containerized applications.
limitations: Does not execute container commands directly
Does not connect to Kubernetes clusters
Requires Docker and Kubernetes CLI tools for actual deployments

useCases.title: Containerize applications
useCases.description: Generate Dockerfiles and compose files for web applications with security best practices

useCases.title: Deploy to Kubernetes
useCases.description: Create production Kubernetes manifests with RBAC, network policies, and autoscaling

useCases.title: Standardize Helm charts
useCases.description: Build reusable Helm chart templates with environment-specific configurations

promptTemplates.title: Create Dockerfile
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a secure Dockerfile for a Python FastAPI application with multi-stage builds, non-root user, and health checks

promptTemplates.title: Docker Compose
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a docker-compose.yml with app, postgres, redis, health checks, and proper networking

promptTemplates.title: K8s Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment with secrets from ConfigMap, resource limits, liveness and readiness probes

promptTemplates.title: Helm Chart
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Helm chart structure with values-staging.yaml and values-production.yaml for different environments

bestPractices: Use specific image versions instead of :latest tags for reproducibility
Run containers as non-root users to limit privilege escalation risks
Add health checks to enable proper container orchestration and restart policies
antiPatterns: Storing secrets in Docker images or environment variables committed to version control
Installing development dependencies in production containers
Using overly permissive base images without minimal attack surface

faq.question: Which Kubernetes versions are supported?
faq.answer: Compatible with Kubernetes 1.25 and later versions with current API versions

faq.question: What are the resource limits for containers?
faq.answer: Recommended starting point: 128Mi memory and 100m CPU, adjust based on application needs

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing Kubernetes clusters?
faq.answer: The skill generates manifests. Apply them using kubectl apply or integrate with GitOps workflows

faq.question: Is my data safe when using these templates?
faq.answer: Templates do not access external systems. Secrets are referenced from Kubernetes Secrets, not hardcoded

faq.question: Why is my container failing health checks?
faq.answer: Ensure the health check endpoint returns HTTP 200. Check that the port matches your application configuration

faq.question: How is this different from plain Docker commands?
faq.answer: This skill provides orchestration patterns for production deployments including scaling, service discovery, and rolling updates
